title: elytrigia repens subsp. pseudocaesia
intro: Elytrigia repens subsp. pseudocaesia, also known as creeping meadow-grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Elytrigia repens subsp. pseudocaesia include soothing digestive problems, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
